scenario: A team examines DMN XML and sees multiple namespaces declared: standard DMN, FEEL, and a Zeebe-specific one. They want to understand the namespace structure.
question: What namespaces are used in Camunda 8 DMN XML?

options.text: Camunda 8 DMN XML uses a single proprietary Camunda namespace for all elements -- this description does not reflect the actual behaviour. Camunda 8 DMN follows the OMG DMN specification and uses multiple layered namespaces. The main DMN elements like definitions, decision, and decisionTable use the OMG DMN namespace. Visual layout information uses the DMNDI namespace. Zeebe-specific execution extensions use the Camunda Zeebe namespace. This layered approach maintains specification compliance while supporting engine-specific features.

Camunda 8 DMN uses flat XML without namespace declarations, relying on element names alone to distinguish DMN, FEEL, and execution-specific elements -- this description does not reflect the actual behaviour. XML namespaces are a fundamental XML standard that all compliant XML documents must support, and Camunda 8 DMN files use multiple namespace declarations in the definitions root element. The OMG DMN namespace, DMNDI diagram interchange namespace, and Camunda Zeebe extension namespace are all declared with their respective namespace prefix bindings.

Multiple namespaces compose the Camunda 8 DMN XML: the OMG DMN namespace for core decision elements such as definitions, decision, and decisionTable; the DMNDI namespace for diagram interchange visual layout positioning data; the FEEL namespace for expression language constructs; and the Camunda Zeebe namespace for engine-specific extension elements. This layered namespace architecture enables full specification compliance for all OMG-namespaced elements while still allowing Zeebe-specific execution hints through the vendor extension namespace without breaking DMN spec conformance.

DMN XML uses exactly the same namespace declarations as BPMN XML since both are Camunda 8 modelling languages -- this description does not reflect the actual behaviour. While both BPMN and DMN files use a Camunda Zeebe extension namespace and both use OMG-defined diagram interchange namespaces, the primary element namespaces differ fundamentally. BPMN uses the OMG BPMN namespace for process elements while DMN uses the OMG DMN namespace for decision elements. Each modelling language has its own distinct primary namespace.

a.text: Incorrect. Camunda 8 DMN XML follows the OMG DMN spec, using multiple namespaces: http://www.omg.org/spec/DMN/...: the main DMN namespace; contains , , , , etc. http://www.omg.org/spec/DMN/.../FEEL: FEEL namespace for expression elements. http://www.omg.org/spec/DMN/.../DMNDI: DMN Diagram Interchange -- visual layout (positions of nodes, edge waypoints, etc.). http://camunda.org/schema/zeebe/...: Camunda Zeebe extensions -- execution-specific metadata for Zeebe. This layered design: Spec compliance: the OMG-namespaced parts are portable across DMN-compliant engines. Vendor extensions: Zeebe-specific runtime hints go in the Zeebe namespace without polluting OMG elements. Diagram preservation: DMNDI captures layout for tools to render the DRD consistently. Parallel: BPMN XML similarly mixes OMG BPMN namespace + Zeebe extension namespace + BPMNDI (diagram interchange).
